questions.question_stem: A 52-year-old man with IgG lambda multiple myeloma and high-risk cytogenetics (deletion 17p) was initially treated with a quadruplet induction with CD38 antibody, bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone followed by high dose melphalan and ASCT and achieved a complete response. He was subsequently on maintenance therapy with daratumumab and lenalidomide and relapsed about 12 months after the ASCT. Which one of the following treatment options are FDA approved for this indication?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Ciltacabtagene autoleucel", "Elranatamab", "Talquetamab", "Teclistamab"]

questions.question_stem: A 68-year-old patient with R/R MM complicated by recurrent episodes of pneumonia is considering treatment with a BCMA-targeted bispecific antibody. Real-world studies suggest that which of the following proactive measures may decrease their chances of risk of infection?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Faster transition to Q2W dosing", "More gradual step-up dosing regimen", "Prophylactic G-CSF"]

questions.question_stem: Populations studies have found that, compared to White patients, Black and Hispanic patients with multiple myeloma experience which of the following?
questions.correct_answer: Longer time from diagnosis to treatment initiation
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Higher inpatient hospitalization rates", "Higher rates of receiving triplet therapy", "Longer duration of lenalidomide maintenance", "Similar utilization of stem cell transplantation"]

questions.question_stem: The addition of isatuximab to KRd significantly increased MRD-negativity rates in TE-NDMM in which of the following cohorts of patients?
questions.correct_answer: Standard, High and Ultra High Risk
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Increased MRD negativity was not observed in any of these cohorts", "Standard and High Risk", "Standard Risk Only"]

questions.question_stem: You are starting a patient on isatuximab. Which of the following medications would you NOT expect to see in the order set?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Acetaminophen", "Acyclovir", "Cetirizine", "Diphenhydramine"]

questions.question_stem: • Age 57-67: Annual executive health checks. –Progressive osteoporosis. Vertebral fractures. Underwent T9 and TL2 vertebroplasty. Noted to have ‘stable MGUS’ (M spike 0.8 g/dL) • Age 68:R rib fractures during massage. Elevated calcium, treated with IVF and pamidronate. Further investigations suggested hyperparathyroidism. Undergoes parathyroidectomy. Stormy post-operative course with bleeding, anemia, infections, AKI. In hospital for 2 months. What is your differential diagnosis?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Gaucher disease-related osteoporosis", "Hyper-parathyroidism related osteoporosis", "Myeloma unrelated to Gaucher disease", "Progressive Gaucher disease"]

questions.question_stem: 77 y/o AA female admitted to our cardiology service with the worsening shortness of breath x 2-3 months and +3 edema in bilateral lower extremities, NT-proBNP: 5087 pg/ml, Lambda FLC: 61.78 mg/dl, Kappa FLC WNL, M-spike: 0.8 g/dl. Right heart catheterization with endomyocardial biopsy confirms AL (Lambda) amyloidosis. BMBx: Lambda-restricted clonal plasma cells (20% on core biopsy). How would you treat the patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bortezomib and Dex", "Dara-CyBorDe +ASCT", "Dara-RVD"]

questions.question_stem: •Brad is a 72-year-old male with R/R MM who has had disease progression on 4 prior lines of therapy •Therapeutic history includes exposure to bortezomib, carfilzomib, lenalidomide, pomalidomide, and daratumumab (penta-drug refractory) •PS of 1 •Has a history of a vascular event, cataract surgery, and has a caregiver at home •He is a candidate for BCMA-directed therapy •Brad’s disease is progressing, and your institution does not have a CAR T-cell slot. What is the best next line of therapy as part of standard of care?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Initiate a second autologous stem cell transplant", "Initiate chemotherapy (i.e. VDCEP)", "Initiate therapy with a non-BCMA targeted regimen"]

questions.question_stem: 54-year-old female was evaluated for anemia with Hb of 7.0 g/dL. Her total protein was elevated at 10.2 gm/dL and SPEP showed monoclonal protein of 5.6 g/dL. Other labs show beta-2 microglobulin of 6 mg/dL, albumin 3.2 g/dL. LDH 245 U/L, CrCl 45 mL/min and serum kappa FLC was 240.0 mg/L. She had a bone marrow biopsy showing 62% monoclonal plasma cells, no cytogenetic (FISH/CTG) abnormalities. PET-CT shows multiple bone lesions in vertebrae without EMD. She has an ECOG PS of 1. She was diagnosed with R-ISS stage 3 IgG-kappa myeloma. How do you treat her?
questions.correct_answer: She is deemed transplant-eligible and treatment with D-RVd is appropriate
questions.incorrect_answers: ["She is deemed transplant-eligible and treatment with D-Rd is appropriate", "She is deemed transplant-ineligible and treatment with D-Rd is appropriate", "She is deemed transplant-ineligible and treatment with RVd is appropriate"]

questions.question_stem: 56-year-old male presents for routine medical follow up and is noted to have an elevated total protein. Workup shows the presence of an IGG Kappa paraprotein of 2.4 gm/dl, Free kappa of 250 with lambda of 10 (k/l ratio 25), UPEP is negative. Bone marrow shows 35% clonal plasma cells with normal FISH testing and cytogenetics. PET/CT shows no evidence of bone disease. B2M is 3.4, and albumin is 4.0. Which of the following is not correct?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Enroll on a trial evaluating treatment for SMM", "Observation", "Treat with len or len/dex"]

questions.question_stem: 61-year-old female recently diagnosed with multiple myeloma after presenting with pelvic fracture. You are planning to start bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone. The patient reports history of LE DVT for which she completed 6 months of anticoagulation 2 year prior to the diagnosis. Based on IMPEDE and SAVED risk stratification score she is at high risk of VTE. Which one of the following primary prophylaxis anticoagulation do you recommend?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Apixaban 5 mg twice daily", "Aspirin 100 mg daily", "LMWH 1 mg/kg subcutaneous twice daily", "Rivaroxaban 20 mg daily"]

questions.question_stem: 64 year old female who was diagnosed with MM in 2013. She was noted to have monosomy 13 and no other genetic abnormalities. She first received RVd followed by autologous transplant and lenalidomide maintenance. •Relapsed in 2016 with rising M protein and bone disease. Was treated with Dara Pom Dex. This was followed by a relapse again in 2020. At that time, she received carfilzomib with cyclophosphamideand Dex.•Had disease progression after 18 months. Presented with rising M protein and extramedullary disease. Was noted to have a creatinine clearance of 45. •What are her treatment options? She is now 74 and also has developed diabetes requiring medical treatment (metformin)
questions.correct_answer: CAR T cells: Ide-cel or Cilta-cel
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Belantamab mafodotin", "Hospice", "Melflufen", "Salvage high dose chemotherapy"]

questions.question_stem: 65-year-old male patient with a medical history significant for hypertension (well controlled) presented with high total protein of 9.1 g/dL, normal CBC, and no other symptoms during a routine checkHis PCP ordered work up for MM, and a PET-CT revealed multiple FDG-avid lytic lesions and an IgG lambda of 2.2 g/dL. Patient was referred to a hematologist. A bone marrow biopsy revealed 35% plasma cells with normal cytogenetics and normal risk FISH. How would you treat the patient?
questions.correct_answer: D-RVD followed by ASCT and maintenance
questions.incorrect_answers: ["KRD followed by ASCT and maintenance", "VRD until disease progression", "Wait and watch"]

questions.question_stem: 67 yo fit M diagnosed with ISS stage III MM with standard-risk CGInitial treatment: DRVd therapy; achieved a VGPR and underwent HDM and autograftAfter 18 mos of maintenance DR therapy, he complains of new LBP and M-spike increases consistent with PDWhich would be the most appropriate FDA-approved treatment for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Continue DR but increase lenalidomide to full dose", "Daratumumab, Pomalidomide, Dexamethasone", "Idecabtagene vicleucel (Ide-cel)", "Teclistamab"]

questions.question_stem: 69-year-old female with PMH of GERD was diagnosed with standard risk myeloma in 09/2021. Bone marrow biopsy showed a hypercellular marrow (60% cellularity) with 60% plasma cells.CTG 46 XX, FISH t(11;14) and del 13Revised ISS Stage II IgG kappa myeloma1st line therapy: RVd induction x 4 cycles -> VGPR, complicated by grade 2 peripheral neuropathyASCT in 1st remissionLenalidomide maintenance started 06/2022, currently progressing with a new bone lesionWhat would be the most appropriate next line of therapy?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["CD38 monoclonal antibody/bortezomib/dexamethasone", "CD38 monoclonal antibody/lenalidomide/dexamethasone", "CD38 monoclonal antibody/pomalidomide/dexamethasone", "Venetoclax/dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: 71-year-old AA man diagnosed with standard risk myeloma 10 years ago at age of 61. Line 1: VRD, auto transplant, len maintenance x 3 years – grade 2 neuropathy resolved to grade 1 with decrease in bortezomib dosing, DVT with first cycleLine 3: KCd x 1 yearLine 4: SeliVD x 6 months, grade 3 N/V and hyponatremiaHe has grade 1 neuropathy, well controlled diabetes on oral medication, overweight, mild renal insufficiency with GFR 50 and WBC chronically in the 2.5 to 3 range.ECOG PS 1For an RRMM patient who has had 4 lines of therapy and is now relapsing, which treatment below offers an opportunity for treatment with a high ORR followed by a relatively long treatment-free interval?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["BCMA bispecific", "GPRC5D bispecific", "Selinexor based regimen"]

questions.question_stem: A 2020 meta-analysis of studies examining minimal residual disease (MRD) status and survival outcomes in patients with multiple myeloma (MM) found which of the following to be true in both transplant-eligible and transplant-ineligible patients with newly diagnosed MM and with relapsed remitting MM, in regard to progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS)?
questions.correct_answer: MRD-negative status was associated with a significant improvement in both PFS and OS
questions.incorrect_answers: ["MRD-negative status was associated with a significant improvement only in OS", "MRD-negative status was associated with a significant improvement only in PFS", "No relationship was found between MRD-negative status and either PFS or OS"]

questions.question_stem: A 50-year-old man recently underwent CAR T-cell therapy. He develops grade 2 neurotoxicity 5 days after CAR T-cell infusion. He has no symptoms of CRS. How would you manage this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["High-dose steroids", "Supportive care", "Tocilizumab"]

questions.question_stem: A 50-year-old man with relapsed multiple myeloma is treated with idecabtagene vicleucel (ide-cel). Two days after CAR T-cell infusion, he develops a fever and reports new onset headache. His blood pressure is 105/70 mm/Hg and his SpO2 is 94%. He has no signs of cognitive impairment. How would you manage this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Administer steroids", "Admit to ICU and initiate high-dose steroids", "Give supportive care"]

questions.question_stem: A 51-year-old man who has R-ISS stage II MM with del17p and t(14;16) returns to your clinic after a second consecutive test demonstrates MRD negativity following 2 cycles of consolidation with Dara-KRd (daratumumab plus carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone). He initially received Dara-KRd induction and first achieved MRD negativity after autologous stem cell transplant. Which of the following statements would be most accurate to counsel the patient regarding available clinical trial data on maintenance therapy in similar patient populations?
questions.correct_answer: Observation alone has been associated with an approximate 25% chance of MRD resurgence or disease progression
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Dara-R maintenance for 2 years has been shown to improve PFS compared with observation alone", "Observation alone has been shown to have non-inferior PFS compared with 3 years of R maintenance", "R maintenance until progression has been shown to improve progression-free survival (PFS) compared with observation alone"]

questions.question_stem: A 52-year-old Black woman with early relapsed multiple myeloma is interested in a clinical trial but fails the screening because her ANC is below 1000 cells/uL. Her ANC has been stable throughout the course of her treatment, and she has no history of infections. Which of the following conditions would be appropriate to test for and, if present, may allow for an exemption to participate in the trial?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["HIV", "Lupus", "Sickle cell trait"]

questions.question_stem: A 52-year-old otherwise healthy patient is newly diagnosed with standard risk multiple myeloma. After reading discussions on several support group message boards, they express a strong desire to undergo ASCT and want an induction regimen that maximizes their chance to achieve a deep and durable response. Which of the following approved combinations would be most appropriate?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Isa-Rd", "KCd", "VRd"]

questions.question_stem: A 54-year-old woman, with history of well-controlled diabetes presents with back pain and anemia (Hb 8.8 g/dL). Monoclonal spike level was 4.4 g/dL, immunoglobulin G (IgG) level was 6.4 g/dL, lambda light chain level was 1814 mg/L, and immunofixation (IFE) showed IgG lambda. Beta 2 microglobulin level was 5.6 mg/L, serum albumin level was 3 g/dL, and lactate dehydrogenase level was within the reference range. Corrected calcium level was 11 mg/dL, and serum creatinine level was 1.14 mg/dL. Bone marrow biopsy showed 50-60% lambda restricted plasma cells. Fluorescence in situ hybridization showed del13q14, 1q gain, t(4;14). CT of chest/abdomen/pelvis showed diffuse osseous lytic lesions and multiple vertebral bodies with loss of height. Which of the following treatment approaches is the best option for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib plus lenalidomide", "Daratumumab + cisplatin + lenalidomide + dexamethasone", "Lenalidomide plus dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 55-year-old woman with multiple myeloma (MM) has disease progression on isatuximab-pomalidomide-dexamethasone (DPd). She experiences grade 3 peripheral neuropathy and wants to minimize her risk of exacerbating these symptoms. The phase 3 CANDOR trial indicates which of the following triplet therapies as a good choice for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bortezomib-lenalidomide-dexamethasone (VRd)", "Ixazomib-lenalidomide-dexamethasone (IRd)", "Pomalidomide-cyclophosphamide-dexamethasone (PCd)"]

questions.question_stem: A 56-year-old Hispanic male presents to your clinic with newly diagnosed, high-risk IgG kappa multiple myeloma. He is eager to start the best available treatment, but he wants to avoid transplant. Which of the following statements is the best option to use when counseling this patient?
questions.correct_answer: He may be eligible for a clinical trial evaluating CAR T-cell therapy instead of transplant
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Large, randomized studies suggest that transplant would not improve his outcomes if he achieves MRD negativity after a quadruplet induction regimen", "Transplant has been shown to prolong overall survival more than any other treatment option"]

questions.question_stem: A 56-year-old male patient with penta-refractory myeloma is preparing to start therapy with talquetamab. All of the following are known/expected toxicities of this agent except?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Cytokine-release syndrome (CRS)", "Dermatologic toxicity", "Gastrointestinal toxicity", "Immune effector-cell associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS)"]

questions.question_stem: A 57-year-old Black male with multiple myeloma presents with disease progression after his third line of therapy. He is here with his adult son, who lives at home and carpools to their shared workplace. You have identified a clinical trial that might be a good fit for him. When you briefly discussed clinical trial options following his first relapse, he was not interested at the time. Which of the following approaches would be most appropriate at this time?
questions.correct_answer: Discuss compensation offered by the study for transportation and time off work
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Ask for his son to leave the room so you can privately discuss clinical trial opportunities", "Avoid discussing known side effects of the study drug until he is amenable to participating in the trial", "Proceed with standard-of-care therapy, as further conversations about clinical trials may alienate him"]

questions.question_stem: A 57-year-old male is newly diagnosed with multiple myeloma. His performance status is excellent. His past medical history and comorbidities include bladder cancer, hyperlipidemia, hypertension, basal cell carcinoma, melanoma, shingles, anxiety, insomnia, colon polyp. He was exposed to trichlorethylene in childhood. His current medications are gabapentin, diazepam, hydrocodone, and acyclovir. Social: retired engineer, married, 2 sons (one son 27 years of age, one adopted son 17 years of age), no tobacco history, occasional alcohol. Which of the following is the best option to assess health literacy and the capacity for shared decision making for this patient?
questions.correct_answer: The patient is highly educated with a previous history of cancer, but this is a new diagnosis, and you should assess his and his families' preferences for information and decision making
questions.incorrect_answers: ["The best approach to this scenario is to assume that patient and his caregivers have no information about how to manage the patient health or discuss his health with the healthcare team", "The patient is highly educated with a previous history of cancer and should be well-equipped to engage in shared decision making and health self-management", "The patient is highly educated with a previous history of cancer; therefore, no further assessment of health literacy or decision-making preferences is required"]

questions.question_stem: A 57-year-old woman is diagnosed with Durie-Salmon 3A, ISS-2, and R-ISS 3 multiple myeloma. She presented with a creatinine level of 1.5 and diffuse osteolytic lesions; bone marrow evaluation showed 50% IgG Kappa restricted plasma cells, FISH with 17p deletion, and t(4;14). She is treated with bortezomib-lenalidomide-dexamethasone followed by autologous stem cell transplantation and lenalidomide maintenance at 10 mg daily for 28/28 days. Minimal residual disease (MRD) assessment of the bone marrow aspirate 6 months after starting lenalidomide shows MRD negativity by multiparametric flow cytometry. Response is consistent with MRD negativity stringent complete remission. At month 12 after starting lenalidomide maintenance, there is no change in her serum markers. Bone marrow evaluation for MRD is positive by multiparametric flow. What is the next best step?
questions.correct_answer: No change in management at this time; repeat myeloma markers in 3 months
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Add dexamethasone 40 mg once weekly", "Increase dose of lenalidomide to 25 mg daily", "Repeat the bone marrow aspirate in 1 week \u2013 it may be a false positive", "Start daratumumab + pomalidomide + dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 58-year-old man with IgG kappa MM, triple class refractory and with 4 prior lines of treatment who has recently progressed on daratumumab, pomalidomide, and dexamethasone. He has an excellent PS, and CBC, metabolic panel are within normal limits. Bone marrow biopsy shows 30-40% plasma cells and FISH and cytogenetics show hyperdiploidy. A PET scan shows multiple lytic bone lesions. He is here to discuss next steps in management. Which of the following FDA-approved treatments do not target the B-cell Maturation Antigen?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Ciltacabtagene autoleucel", "Elranatamab", "Idecabtagene vicleucel", "Teclistamab"]

questions.question_stem: A 59-year-old woman with newly diagnosed high-risk smoldering multiple myeloma (SMM) meets Mayo 2018 “20-2-20” criteria. Her FISH shows 1q gain and t(4;14). She is enrolled in a clinical trial evaluating teclistamab. After 5 cycles, she achieves a complete response and MRD negativity at 10⁻⁶. Which of the following best describes the expected efficacy of teclistamab in this setting based on current trial data?
questions.correct_answer: High ORR with deep responses including 100% MRD negativity among evaluable patients
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Low response rate and high discontinuation due to immune-related toxicity", "MRD negativity typically not achieved before 12 cycles of treatment", "Partial response rate of 30% with minimal MRD negativity"]

questions.question_stem: A 60-year-old African American gentleman presents with thoracic back pain. His past medical history is significant for hypertension requiring multiple antihypertensive medications. MRI of the spine shows collapse of T11. He is referred to your oncology clinic and is diagnosed with R-ISS 3 high risk t(11;14) multiple myeloma. While discussing first-line treatment options, the patient is apprehensive about undergoing a stem cell transplant. Which of the following statements would be appropriate to share with the patient at this time?
questions.correct_answer: Transplantation would be a good choice because studies have shown it to be very effective, especially in African American patients; however, it will likely require temporary changes in your blood pressure medications and close monitoring of your blood pressure after the transplant
questions.incorrect_answers: ["It would be wise to avoid stem cell transplantation because a study looking at outcomes in many African American patients have shown transplantation to be less effective than using anti-myeloma medications alone", "It would be wise to avoid stem cell transplantation because studies have shown it is less effective for African American patients with genetic features of your multiple myeloma", "Transplantation would be a good choice because studies have shown it will likely reduce the total costs of your treatment compared with using anti-myeloma medications alone"]

questions.question_stem: A 60-year-old patient with MM has received 5 prior lines of therapy including a proteasome inhibitor, immunomodulatory agent, and anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody (triple-class refractory). He has now relapsed again and is evaluating treatment options. According to NCCN guidelines, which of the following therapies would be most appropriate for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bendamustine/bortezomib/dexamethasone", "Carfilzomib/lenalidomide/dexamethasone (KRd)", "Isatuximab plus KRd or daratumumab + KRd"]

questions.question_stem: A 61-year-old female with IgG kappa myeloma with t(11;14) has progressed through multiple lines of therapy including RVd, ASCT, len maintenance, dara/pom/dex, venetoclax clinical trial, carfilzomib/cyclo/dex. She is now 3 months post CAR-T and progressing with a rise in her M protein and new FDG active lesions on PET/CT. The patient has no other symptoms and a good PS. What is the best option for next treatment?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib/pom/dex", "Consider a second ASCT", "Give another infusion of CAR-T cells"]

questions.question_stem: A 61-year-old woman with Revised-International Staging System (R-ISS) III MM plans to undergo treatment using induction therapy with lenalidomide, bortezomib, dexamethasone, and daratumumab with intention to proceed to ASCT. Which of the following is true regarding daratumumab induction therapy and mobilization for ASCT?
questions.correct_answer: Daratumumab impacts cell yield but not engraftment
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Daratumumab should be avoided, because it negatively impacts CD34+ cell yield and engraftment", "There is no impact on mobilization", "When daratumumab is part of induction therapy, the addition of plerixafor to G-CSF mobilization has no impact on CD34+ cell yield"]

questions.question_stem: A 62-year-old man presents with a 3-month history of fatigue, night sweats, weight loss, and darker urine. He has no history of heart or liver disease. Full blood count revealed hemoglobin 10g/dL, white cell count 6 × 109/L (neutrophils 1+, lymphocytes 2+), thrombocytopenia (platelets 75 × 109/L), serum creatinine 99 μmol/L, urea 4 mmol/L. Serum protein electrophoresis demonstrated an M-spike of 8g/dL with IgGkappa monoclonality, confirming multiple myeloma based on CRAB criteria. Imaging demonstrated lytic lesions in the axial skeleton and bilateral pulmonary opacities consistent with extramedullary plasmacytomas. R-ISS staging indicated intermediate-risk disease. He has stable performance status. Discussion reveals that the patient is open to transplant. Would you consider this patient for transplant?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, his stable performance status and lack of heart disease or cirrhosis qualify him for transplant
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Need more information before deciding", "No, despite his performance status, the lytic lesions in his axial skeleton disqualify him"]

questions.question_stem: A 62-year-old man with t(14:16) IgG kappa multiple myeloma (MM) presents with asymptomatic biochemical relapse while on selinexor, pomalidomide, and dexamethasone. He previously underwent upfront autologous stem cell transplant with VRd induction/consolidation and received isatuximab, carfilzomib, and dexamethasone after his first relapse. Which of the following would be most appropriate at this time?
questions.correct_answer: Offer referral to CAR T-cell specialist
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Start elranatamab or teclistamab", "Start talquetamab", "Start venetoclax plus bortezomib and dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 62-year-old patient with IgG Kappa multiple myeloma has progressed beyond 4 lines of prior therapy. She is given a BCMA x CD3 bispecific antibody. She tolerated the infusion well on C1D1 and is admitted to the hospital for observation. On C1D2 she reports feeling warm and unwell. Temperature is 102.1. BP 89/57. Pulse 117. O2 sat 91% on room air. She is placed on oxygen via nasal cannula with improvement in saturation to 96%. Along with supportive measures, what therapy should be administered next?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Anakinra", "Entanercept", "IVIG", "Rituximab"]

questions.question_stem: A 62-year-old patient with R-ISS stage 3 IgG kappa MM is MRD positive following VRd induction and ASCT. Adding which of the following treatments to lenalidomide maintenance would be most appropriate based on evidence from a randomized phase 3 clinical trial?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib", "Ciltacabtagene autoleucel", "Isatuximab", "Teclistamab"]

questions.question_stem: A 62-year-old woman was noted to have elevated total protein on routine evaluation -> SPEP demonstrated a monoclonal protein of 3.1 gm/dL, IgA kappa. Hgb was 13 g/dL, serum calcium, and creatinine were normal. Serum-free light chain assay showed elevated kappa of 13 mg/dL, lambda of 0.8 mg/dL, and FLC ratio of 16. A whole-body low-dose CT scan showed no lytic lesions. Bone marrow examination showed 30% plasma cells, and FISH tests showed the presence of t(4;14).What would be an appropriate next step?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Order return visit with labs and imaging in 1 year", "Repeat labs in 3 months", "Start lenalidomide and dexamethasone", "Start treatment with Dara-VRd"]

questions.question_stem: A 63-year-old gentleman who has previously received numerous therapies for multiple myeloma presents with relapsing disease. Following discussions with his care team, he opts to receive a BCMA CAR-T as his next line of therapy. Which of the following statements is NOT correct?
questions.correct_answer: Do not proceed with this approach as his disease is too refractory to therapy and so CAR-T cell therapy is unlikely to be beneficial
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bridging chemotherapy to help with disease control whilst the CAR-T cell product is being manufactured may be required", "Close monitoring of temperature, heart rate, and blood pressure post-CAR-T infusion is required. Swift intervention with an anti-IL6 antibody at first sign of grade 2 CRS would be appropriate", "Daily neurological examination including assessment of naming objects and writing post-CAR-T infusion is required"]

questions.question_stem: A 64 year old male patient calls into the hem/onc clinic to update the team. He received his second full dose of teclistamab two days prior and has developed a pruritic rash across his back and chest starting last night. He also notes mild sore throat and congestion developing this morning. He denies any fever or shortness of breath. He is A & O x 4. Do you advise the patient report to the ED for CRS concerns?
questions.correct_answer: No, but consider other work up
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Yes"]

questions.question_stem: A 64-year-old man is diagnosed with revised international staging system (R-ISS) stage II multiple myeloma (standard-risk cytogenetics). Comorbidities include well-controlled hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Based on current evidence, which of the following induction regimens would be most appropriate prior to autologous stem cell transplant (ASCT)?
questions.correct_answer: An anti-CD38 mAb (isatuximab or daratumumab) with VRd
questions.incorrect_answers: ["An anti-CD38 mAb with cyclophosphamide, bortezomib, and dexamethasone (CyBorD)", "Bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone (VRd)", "Lenalidomide and dexamethasone (Rd)"]

questions.question_stem: A 64-year-old man with standard risk newly diagnosed MM has been treated with treated with bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone for 1 year. He asks you about the utility of undergoing a bone biopsy to assess his minimal residual disease (MRD) status. Which of the following statements would be most appropriate to share with him at this time?
questions.correct_answer: Testing for MRD status can provide information about your prognosis, but there is not enough clinical data to use the results to make any changes to your current treatment
questions.incorrect_answers: ["I would recommend testing for MRD status, because the results of a recent randomized trial showed that we should add an anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody to your treatment regimen if you are MRD positive", "I would recommend testing for MRD status, because the results of several recent randomized trials have shown that we can safely discontinue your treatment if you are MRD negative"]

questions.question_stem: A 64-year-old woman has R-ISS stage II MM with t(4;14) detected by fluorescence in situ hybridization. She presents with new lytic lesions in her spine while on pomalidomide plus bortezomib and dexamethasone. She underwent ASCT 4 years ago with daratumumab plus bortezomib and lenalidomide induction/consolidation, and her disease progressed on lenalidomide maintenance. Which one of the following treatments would you recommend as the next therapeutic option?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Belantamab mafodotin plus lenalidomide and dexamethasone", "Carfilzomib plus lenalidomide and dexamethasone", "Venetoclax plus dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 65-year-old man is diagnosed with Revised International Staging System (R-ISS) stage II multiple myeloma (MM) (standard-risk cytogenetics). Comorbidities include well-controlled hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Based on current evidence, which of the following induction regimens would be most appropriate?
questions.correct_answer: An anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody with VRd
questions.incorrect_answers: ["An anti-CD38 mAb with cyclophosphamide, bortezomib, and dexamethasone (CyBorD)", "Bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone (VRd)", "Carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone (KRd)", "Lenalidomide and dexamethasone (Rd)"]

questions.question_stem: A 65-year-old man with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma presents with rapid progression after 3 months of carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone. PET scan reveals multiple new liver lesions, which are confirmed to be extramedullary plasmacytomas on biopsy. Based on results from a phase 1b trial, a clinical trial with which of the following treatments would be most appropriate for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Alnuctamab plus mezigdomide", "Elranatamab plus daratumumab", "Linvoseltamab plus isatuximab"]

questions.question_stem: A 65-year-old patient with del(17p) IgG kappa multiple myeloma has a very good partial response with detectable MRD after Dara-VRd induction and ASCT. Which of the following maintenance strategies has been shown to improve rates of deep and durable responses?
questions.correct_answer: Lenalidomide until disease progression plus daratumumab for at least 2 years
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Lenalidomide for at least 2 years", "Lenalidomide plus daratumumab until disease progression", "Lenalidomide until disease progression"]

questions.question_stem: A 65-year-old woman presented over the summer with back pain progressive. Had initial workup with chiropractor with no relief in symptoms. Blood counts showed mild anemia (Hgb of 10.5), normal WBC and plts. Chemistries demonstrated normal renal function, calcium, and LDH. SPEP demonstrated m spike of 0, but free kappa of 2500, and lambda of 10 for abnormal ratio. Marrow demonstrated 30% clonal plasma cells FISH was +9, and +11. B2M was 4.1, albumin was 3.7. Imaging demonstrated lytic disease in the spine using PET/CT. What is the stage for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["ISS stage 1", "ISS stage 3", "RISS stage 1"]

questions.question_stem: A 67-year-old African American man with MM presents with a new lytic bone lesion in his spine while on carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone. His previous treatment history includes induction therapy with lenalidomide, bortezomib, and dexamethasone followed by ASCT, with prior relapse on lenalidomide maintenance. Which of the following options would be most appropriate for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Belantamab mafodotin", "Selinexor and dexamethasone", "Venetoclax and dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 68 year-old white man presents to the ER with weakness and thoracic back pain. X-ray showed a 2 cm T6 lytic lesion. Further investigation and lab work showed: IgG > 6 g/dL; kappa light chains > 300 g/dL; M spike of 2.3 g/dL; Hgb 9 g/dL; creatinine 2.4 mg/dL; GFR 62; B2m 5.6 mg/dL; albumin 3.1 mMol/dL; calcium 9.7 mg/dL. Bone marrow biopsy: 80% plasma cells, FISH t(11;14).Diagnosis: IgG kappa multiple myeloma, R-ISS stage III. How many drugs do clinical trial data suggest should be first-line therapy for this transplant-eligible patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["One", "Three", "Two"]

questions.question_stem: A 68-year-old female with R/R MM with 8 prior lines of therapy recently relapsed off a BCMA CAR T and will now begin therapy on talquetamab. When considering talquetamab for patients it is important to consider:
questions.correct_answer: Talquetamab targets GPRC5D which is present on the MM cells as well as keratinized cells which can lead to off target side effects
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Talquetamab targets BCMA and patients will be at high risk for infection", "Talquetamab targets BCMA and places patients high risk for high grades of CRS", "Talquetamab targets GPRC5D which is expressed on MM cells and cardiac cells. Patients with cardiac disease should avoid treatment"]

questions.question_stem: A 68-year-old man with multiple myeloma (MM) is refractory to lenalidomide, pomalidomide, bortezomib, carfilzomib, and daratumumab (penta-refractory). Cytogenetic evaluation finds that his MM is positive for t(14;16) and negative for del(17p), t(4;14), and t(11:14). Of the following treatment regimens, which would be most appropriate?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib/dexamethasone", "Daratumumab/pomalidomide/dexamethasone", "Venetoclax/bortezomib/dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 68-year-old patient with R-ISS Stage I MM achieves a VGPR after transplant following bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone (VRd) induction. Based on available data, which of the following would be the most appropriate approach to maintenance therapy?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Daratumumab", "Lenalidomide plus bortezomib", "Lenalidomide plus isatuximab", "Observation"]

questions.question_stem: A 68-year-old white man was diagnosed 6 years ago with IgA lambda multiple myeloma. He is refractory to lenalidomide, pomalidomide, bortezomib, carfilzomib, daratumumab, and cyclophosphamide. He was most recently treated with isatuximab, pomalidomide and dexamethasone. He also has hypertension and higher-risk cytogenetics. The patient now presents with disease progression. Which of the following treatments do you select?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Elotuzomab", "Selinexor/carfilzomib/dexamethasone", "Venetoclax/dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 69-year-old man is treated for Durie-Salmon 3A, ISS-2, t(11;14) myeloma with upfront carfilzomib, lenalidomide, dexamethasone followed by autologous stem cell transplantation and declines lenalidomide maintenance. He is found to have relapsed disease 4 years later with rising M-spike and new FDG-avid vertebral lytic disease with extramedullary right rib plasmacytoma on PET/CT. He has family support and lives near the office. Comorbidities include type 2 DM with underlying mild peripheral neuropathy, OSA, and GERD. All of the following would be appropriate treatment options except:
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib-daratumumab-dexamethasone", "Daratumumab-lenalidomide-dexamethasone", "Daratumumab-pomalidomide-dexamethasone", "Isatuximab-carfilzomib-dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 69-year-old man presents with newly diagnosed IgG kappa multiple myeloma with standard-risk cytogenetics and ECOG performance status 1. He has controlled hypertension and mild COPD on PRN albuterol. He lives independently but requires occasional help with household tasks and has had recent unintentional weight loss. Which of the following best describes his transplant eligibility?
questions.correct_answer: Further frailty assessment is needed to adequately assess his transplant eligibility
questions.incorrect_answers: ["He is unlikely to benefit from upfront transplant because he has standard-risk cytogenetics", "His age exceeds the typical upper limit for transplant eligibility", "His ECOG performance status makes him ineligible for transplant", "The presence of COPD is an absolute contraindication to high-dose melphalan and stem cell transplant"]

questions.question_stem: A 69-year-old patient in your primary care practice has relapsed MM after 3 lines of treatment. Her hematologist has started her on isatuximab, carfilzomib and dexamethasone therapy. She asks you if this new regimen will help her. Which of the following would be appropriate information to share?
questions.correct_answer: PFS is about 3 years in all patients
questions.incorrect_answers: ["PFS is about 1 year in patients with a good prognosis", "PFS is about 3 years in patients with a good prognosis", "PFS is not known"]

questions.question_stem: A 71-year-old man with HTN and A fib is diagnosed with ISS stage 3 IgG kappa myeloma with standard risk CG and extensive bone disease including T6 fracture. Good response but poor tolerance to CyBorD, Rd, DPd, and Kd. He now presents for CAR T-cell therapy. He received LD and T cells on schedule. On day 7, he developed a temperature of 102.1°F and BP 82/55. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Acyclovir", "Anakinra", "Tocilizumab"]

questions.question_stem: A 71-year-old man with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma is starting daratumumab, carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone. He expresses concern about diarrhea after reading about lenalidomide and admits he sometimes forgets medications. As the research nurse preparing him for treatment adherence, which intervention best supports adherence and quality of life?
questions.correct_answer: Educate the patient on early reporting of diarrhea, recommend antidiarrheal therapy, and set up reminders for daily dosing
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Encourage holding doses during weekends to avoid side effects", "Immediately reduce lenalidomide dose", "Reassure the patient diarrhea will resolve on its own and encourage strict adherence"]

questions.question_stem: A 72-year-old male with a long history of IgA kappa myeloma has had multiple lines of therapy and is currently progressing two years post CAR-T. Treatment with the GPRD5D bispecific antibody talquetamab is considered. The ORR in patients who have had prior CAR-T therapy based on the MonumenTAL-1 clinical trial is which of the following?

A 72-year-old patient in your practice recently started carfilzomib treatment for multiple myeloma. Which of the following symptoms will you ask him about to determine if he is experiencing adverse events associated with the drug?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Hearing loss", "Increased sweating", "Increased urination"]

questions.question_stem: A 72-year-old patient who is currently being treated with pomalidomide, bortezomib, and dexamethasone as fourth-line therapy for multiple myeloma is found to have an 0.3 g/dL increase in serum M-protein but has no new symptoms. He is otherwise fit, has family near to the closest academic hospital center, and is eager to receive the most effective treatment for his next relapse. Now would be the most appropriate time to discuss which of the following treatment options?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bendamustine plus lenalidomide and dexamethasone", "Elranatamab or teclistamab", "Selinexor plus carfilzomib and dexamethasone", "Talquetamab"]

questions.question_stem: A 72-year-old woman with t(14:16) IgG kappa multiple myeloma presents with rapid disease progression while on KRd. This is now her 4th relapse. Her treatment course has been complicated by peripheral neuropathy and 10-pound weight loss. She is a retired chef and enjoys cooking meals for her grandchildren. Which of the following treatments would be most appropriate to start at this time?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bendamustine", "Cilta-cel or ide-cel", "Talquetamab", "Venetoclax-Vd"]

questions.question_stem: A 73-year-old Hispanic patient is newly diagnosed with ISS stage II IgG kappa multiple myeloma. He is ineligible for ASCT, but has normal renal and pulmonary function. Which of the following treatments would be the best option for this patient?

questions.question_stem: A 73-year-old patient with standard-risk multiple myeloma (MM) returns for normal follow-up 12 months after starting maintenance therapy with daratumumab and lenalidomide. He previously achieved minimal residual disease (MRD) negativity following quadruplet induction and autologous stem cell transplant. The patient is interested in discontinuing maintenance and wonders if testing for MRD could help guide that decision. Which of the following statements would be most appropriate to share with the patient?
questions.correct_answer: There currently are no data from randomized clinical trials to support the use of MRD status to guide the decision in this situation
questions.incorrect_answers: ["There are data from randomized clinical trials suggesting that continuing treatment could lead to prolonged OS if he were MRD positive", "There are data from randomized clinical trials suggesting that continuing treatment could lead to prolonged OS regardless of MRD status", "There are data from randomized clinical trials suggesting that stopping treatment would not impact survival outcomes if he were MRD negative"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Discontinue anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody after the first 18 cycles", "Discontinue lenalidomide after the first 18 cycles", "Use carfilzomib as the proteasome inhibitor backbone"]

questions.question_stem: A 77-year-old Hispanic woman with t(14:16) IgG kappa MM presents with asymptomatic biochemical relapse while on ixazomib-pomalidomide-dexamethasone. She previously progressed on first-line treatment with bortezomib-lenalidomide and second-line daratumumab-carfilzomib-dexamethasone. questions.correct_answer: Offer a referral to CAR T-cell specialist
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Start elranatamab or teclistamab", "Start talquetamab", "Start venetoclax and dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 78-year-old Black male with a past medical history significant for congestive heart failure and chronic kidney disease presents to your clinic with newly diagnosed R-ISS stage II IgG kappa multiple myeloma. questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone", "Carfilzomib, cyclophosphamide, and dexamethasone", "Reduced-dose melphalan and ASCT"]

questions.question_stem: A 78-year-old Hispanic man is newly diagnosed with International Staging System stage II immunoglobulin G–κ multiple myeloma (MM). He is ineligible for autologous stem cell transplant (ASCT), and he has normal renal and pulmonary function. Which of the following treatments would be the best option for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone", "Daratumumab, bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone", "Lenalidomide and dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 78-year-old man is newly diagnosed with ISS stage II IgG kappa multiple myeloma. He is ineligible for autologous stem cell transplant (ASCT) and has normal renal and pulmonary function. Which of the following treatments would be the best option for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone", "Elotuzumab, carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone", "Lenalidomide and dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A 78-year-old man presents to your clinic with newly diagnosed R-ISS stage II immunoglobulin G kappa (IgGκ) multiple myeloma (MM). Initial results from a phase 3 study suggest that which of the following treatments might be an effective steroid-sparing option for this patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib + lenalidomide", "Dexamethasone is needed to maximize chance of achieving treatment response and MRD negativity", "Isatuximab + bortezomib + lenalidomide"]

questions.question_stem: A 78-year-old man with NDMM is referred to your clinic. He is wheelchair bound and lives with his family for supportive care (IFM frailty score 4). His significant comorbidities include a history of MI 3 years ago (current EF 45%) and diabetic nephropathy (CrCl 35 mL/min). The patient and his family are concerned about potential treatment toxicities. Which of the following treatments has been shown to be tolerable and most effective in this population?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Daratumumab plus lenalidomide and dexamethasone (Dara-Rd)", "Isatuximab plus lenalidomide and dexamethasone (Isa-Rd)", "Lenalidomide plus low-dose dexamethasone"]

Phase 2 data suggest that which of the following approaches may improve convenience without sacrificing disease control?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Discontinue bortezomib", "Discontinue dexamethasone", "Switch bortezomib to belantamab mafodotin"]

questions.question_stem: A 78-year-old patient with triple-class refractory MM is planning to start elranatamab. Their treatment course has been complicated by recurrent anemia, peripheral neuropathy, skin rash, and COVID-19 infection. They are currently asymptomatic, and their neutrophil count is normal. In addition to prophylaxis against PJP and VZV, which of the following prophylactic treatments would you recommend?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Dexamethasone", "Levofloxacin", "Tocilizumab"]

questions.question_stem: A 78-year-old woman was scored as high-risk for SMM by all assessed models conducted. She has expressed a desire for treatment to her hematologist. There are no clinical trials available to her that fit her needs. According to the National Comprehensive Cancer Network guidelines, which of the following treatments may be considered?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bortezomib monotherapy", "Carfilzomib monotherapy", "Due to her age, treatment is not recommended", "Guidelines only include observation as an option (there are no listed treatments)"]

questions.question_stem: A 78-year-old, otherwise fit patient with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma (NDMM) undergoes a bone marrow biopsy; the results reveal 2 high-risk cytogenetic abnormalities. After shared decision-making discussions, they do not wish to undergo up-front autologous stem cell transplantation (ASCT). Which of the following is a NCCN guideline–preferred regimen that would maximize their chance to achieve a deep response?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Dara-Rd (daratumumab/lenalidomide/dexamethasone)", "KRd (carfilzomib/lenalidomide/dexamethasone)", "VRd (bortezomib/lenalidomide/dexamethasone)"]

questions.question_stem: A fit 70-year-old patient with newly diagnosed IgG kappa multiple myeloma, R-ISS stage II, undergoes induction with 4 cycles of bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone (VRD) and achieves a complete remission. The patient now comes to you for a second opinion on whether to pursue high-dose chemotherapy followed by autologous stem cell transplantation at this time. Which of the following statements is true?
questions.correct_answer: This patient’s age does not exclude the possibility of transplant
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Achieving a deeper remission will not be possible with transplant", "If deferred, transplant will no longer be an option in the future", "Transplant will be unlikely to prolong time to progression"]

questions.question_stem: A man aged 55 years with R/R MM has relapsed after 5 prior therapies, including a BCMA-targeted antibody-drug conjugate as the latest treatment. Which of the following eligibility criteria for CAR T-cell therapy is important to deciding if he is a candidate for CAR T-cell therapy?
questions.correct_answer: His history of previous BCMA-targeted therapy
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Duration of time since his diagnosis of multiple myeloma", "His age", "If he has traditional, measurable disease"]

questions.question_stem: A patient with relapsed refractory multiple myeloma after previous treatment with a proteasome inhibitor, an immunomodulatory drug, and an anti-CD38 antibody was started on elranatamab. According to the Magnetismm-3 trial, what is his risk of developing grade 1 or 2 CRS?

A patient with standard-risk multiple myeloma achieved a complete response and MRD negativity following VRd induction and ASCT. After 12 months of lenalidomide maintenance, repeat MRD testing shows detectable disease at 10-5. Their labs are otherwise normal, and they are asymptomatic. Which of the following is currently the most appropriate evidence-based treatment approach?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Add anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody", "Add carfilzomib", "Switch to anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody plus carfilzomib or pomalidomide and dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: A patient, aged 63 years, with relapsed MM is discussing therapy with a bispecific agent. Which of the following is an adverse event of interest to discuss with the patient?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Atrial fibrillation", "Interstitial lung disease"]

questions.question_stem: A phase 1 study of etentamig (ABBV-383) in patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma found that only 30% of patients experienced any grade CRS with which of the following mitigation strategies?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["2 progressive step-up doses with tocilizumab premedication", "3 progressive step-up doses with longer dosing intervals", "4 progressive step-up doses"]

questions.question_stem: A study by Aschauer et al found which of the following changes in corneal subbasal nerves in patients undergoing treatment with belantamab mafodotin?
questions.correct_answer: Evidence of complete loss of the subbasal nerve plexus
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Increased density of subbasal nerves", "Potential loss of subbasal nerves, though corneal confocal microscopy was not sensitive enough to assess the degree of damage", "Temporary reduction in subbasal nerve density"]

questions.question_stem: A transplant-eligible patient with NDMM is considering between quadruplet and triplet therapy. You should inform them that treatment with an anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody increases the chances that they will require which of the following medications prior to transplant?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Chlorothiazide", "Gabapentin", "Ondansetron"]

questions.question_stem: A transplant-ineligible patient with high-risk NDMM achieved minimal residual disease (MRD) negativity at the end of induction with a quadruplet regimen. They return after 12 months to discuss the utility of undergoing another MRD test. They previously stopped the dexamethasone; they are currently taking an anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody with lenalidomide. Which of the following approach is most appropriate based on currently available evidence?
questions.correct_answer: Continue anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody and lenalidomide regardless of MRD status
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Discontinue all treatment if MRD negative", "Discontinue only anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody if MRD negative", "Discontinue only lenalidomide if MRD negative"]

questions.question_stem: According to a recent review by Mikhael and colleagues, what is the average time between a patient with symptoms of MM presenting to a primary care physician and a diagnosis of MM?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["1 month", "12 months", "24 months"]

questions.question_stem: According to an analysis of the SEER database, Black patients with multiple myeloma were how many times less likely to use ASCT compared with White patients?
According to the 2014 population-based NHANES study of 12,482 persons, which racial/ethnic group had the highest prevalence of monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Asian/Pacific Islander patients", "Hispanic patients", "White patients"]

questions.question_stem: All of the following are key targets in treating myeloma except?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["BCMA", "CD38", "FcRH5", "GPRC5d"]

questions.question_stem: An analysis of long-term outcomes from a randomized phase 3 study demonstrated the benefit of adding which of the following treatments to carfilzomib and dexamethasone for patients with high-risk 1q21+ chromosomal abnormalities?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Daratumumab", "Elotuzumab", "Selinexor", "Venetoclax"]

questions.question_stem: Analysis of Surveillance, Epidemiology, and End Results data assessing Medicare beneficiaries diagnosed with multiple myeloma (MM) from 2007 to 2017 observed which of the following trends among Black patients?
questions.correct_answer: Increasing rates of treatment delays from initial diagnosis
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Decreasing rates of diagnostic delays from first presentation to primary care provider or emergency department", "Decreasing use of anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody\u2013based triplets", "Increasing use of autologous stem cell transplant (ASCT)"]

questions.question_stem: Approximately what percentage of patients were progression free at 2 years in the CARTITUDE-1 trial, which evaluated the B-cell maturation antigen (BCMA)-directed CAR T-cell therapy ciltacabtagene autoleucel for heavily pretreated patients with MM?

Available evidence from real world studies and post-hoc analyses of clinical trials suggest that which of the following sequences of T-cell redirecting therapies may maximize efficacy in patients with relapsed/refractory MM?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Anti-BCMA therapy before anti-GPRC5D therapy", "Anti-GPRC5D therapy before an anti-BCMA therapy", "Bispecific antibody before CAR-T therapy"]

questions.question_stem: Based on results from a retrospective study of VA data, how do survival outcomes of Black patients compare to those of White patients when Black patients have equal access to care?
questions.correct_answer: Better survival outcomes for Black patients
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Same survival outcomes", "Worse survival outcomes for Black patients"]

questions.question_stem: Based on the DREAMM-7 trial, belantamab mafodotin gained FDA approval as a combination with which of the following therapies?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Carfilzomib-dexamethasone", "Dexamethasone", "Lenalidomide-dexamethasone"]

questions.question_stem: Besides CRS and ICANS what is a common dose limiting toxicity associated with bispecific antibodies in multiple myeloma?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Cardiotoxicity", "Increased liver enzymes", "Nausea and vomiting"]

questions.question_stem: Both FDA-approved CAR T-cell therapies and 2 out of the 3 approved bispecific antibodies target which of the following antigen on myeloma cells?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["CD38", "FCRH", "GPRC5D"]

questions.question_stem: CAR T-cell therapies are currently indicated for patients with MM who have had prior treatment with a proteasome inhibitor, an immunomodulatory agent, and an anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody and who have received at least how many prior lines of therapy?

Chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy (ie, idecabtagene vicleucel, ciltacabtagene autoleucel) is currently available for patients with MM who have received an IMiD, a PI, and an anti-CD38 mAb and how many prior lines of therapy?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["At least 2", "At least 3", "At least 5"]

questions.question_stem: Compared to white patients, which of the following is true regarding Black patients with MM?
questions.correct_answer: They have more comorbidities at presentation
questions.incorrect_answers: ["They are well represented in clinical trials", "They have a lower prevalence of MM", "They present at older ages"]

questions.question_stem: CRS is characterized by extreme inflammatory response triggered by increased IL-2, IL-6, tumor necrosis factor-alpha and other inflammatory cytokines. ____ is a required initial finding to diagnose CRS of any grade.
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Hypotension", "Neutropenia", "Rash"]

questions.question_stem: Currently available chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapies for multiple myeloma (MM) utilize which of the following targets?
Differences in the immune bone marrow microenvironment may be associated with which of the following among Black patients as compared with White patients?
questions.correct_answer: Increased rates of cytokine release syndrome following chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Decreased response rates to anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody-based quadruplet regimens", "Decreased response rates to T-cell redirecting therapies", "Increased rates of early disease relapse after ASCT"]

questions.question_stem: During teclistamab therapy which of the following is recommended?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Ice chips to prevent mucosal sores", "Measles, Mumps, rubella vaccination updated", "Prophylactic kytril and emend to prevent nausea"]

questions.question_stem: For the treatment of patients with multiple myeloma (MM), CAR T-cells are engineered to target which of the following antigens on malignant cell membranes?

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Hospitalization after the first infusion is required to monitor for CRS and neurotoxicity", "Infusions must be administered at a specialized medical center", "Treatment may enhance efficacy of later-line CAR-T therapy"]

questions.question_stem: In a phase 2 dose-expansion study of the CELMoD mezigdomide plus dexamethasone for patients with triple class-refractory MM, what was the overall response rate in the 30 patients who had prior exposure to a BCMA-targeted therapy?

In a phase 2, dose-expansion study of the cereblon E3 ligase modulator mezigdomide plus dexamethasone given to patients with triple class–refractory MM, what was the overall response rate in the 30 patients who had prior exposure to a BCMA-targeted therapy?

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Idecabtagene vicleucel", "Talquetamab", "Teclistamab"]

questions.question_stem: In a real-world analysis of 207 patients who received standard of care idecabtagene vicleucel across 11 US sites, which of the following patient populations had the lowest overall response rate to this CAR-T therapy?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Non-Hispanic Black patients", "Non-Hispanic White patients"]

questions.question_stem: In a real-world analysis of 215 patients with R/R MM who received standard of care ide-cel across 11 US sites, which of the following was significantly increased in Black compared to White patients?

questions.question_stem: In a real-world analysis of 215 patients with relapsed/refractory MM who received standard of care idecabtagene vicleucel across 11 US sites, which of the following was significantly increased in Black patients compared with White patients?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Incidence of grade 3 thrombocytopenia", "Median progression-free survival", "Rate of any-grade immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS)"]

questions.question_stem: In a retrospective analysis of outcomes among Black patients in the phase 3 DETERMINATION trial, which of the following was predictive of a lack of benefit for bortezomib/lenalidomide/dexamethasone (RVd) + ASCT compared with RVd alone?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Age \u2265 60", "High-risk cytogenetics on FISH", "Male gender"]

questions.question_stem: In patients with multi-refractory multiple myeloma who are being considered for CAR T-cell therapy, which of the following drug should NOT be given prior to CAR T-cell therapy?

questions.question_stem: In patients with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma (NDMM), the best PFS outcomes are seen with which of the following therapies (select the best response)?

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Lower rate of patients achieving MRD negativity", "No significant effect"]

A bone marrow biopsy revealed a hypercellular marrow with 60% plasma cell involvement. PET/CT scan reveals multiple hypermetabolic osseous lesions in both the axial and appendicular skeleton. questions.incorrect_answers: ["del(17p)", "del(1p)", "gain/amp(1q21)"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Anti-CD38 mAbs", "CELMoDs", "GPRC5D-targeted BsAbs", "XPO1 inhibitors"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["CRS", "ICANs", "Infections", "Neurotoxicity"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["CEA", "iRNA", "PDL-1"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is a therapeutic target of an FDA-approved BiTE for Multiple Myeloma?

questions.correct_answer: BCMA has lower expression in healthy tissues, such as skin and nails

questions.correct_answer: BCMA has lower expression in healthy tissue in the skin, nail, and tongue
questions.incorrect_answers: ["BCMA target loss is a common mechanism of resistance that does not occur with targeting GPCR5D", "GPCR5D expression is dependent on BCMA expression, but not vice versa", "GPCR5D is more frequently expressed in myeloma cells"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is an important distinction between BCMA and GPRC5D as a therapeutic target for multiple myeloma?
questions.correct_answer: BCMA has lower expression in healthy skin and nail tissues
questions.incorrect_answers: ["BCMA-targeted therapies are associated with lower rates of infection", "GPRC5D expression is dependent on BCMA expression, but not vice versa", "GPRC5D is more frequently expressed on myeloma cells"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Patient without ultra-high risk markers", "Patients over age 50 years", "Patients with comorbidities"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is NOT an FDA–AACR recommendation for eliminating racial disparities in multiple myeloma therapies?
questions.correct_answer: Trial design should encompass disease subtypes and features most commonly seen in the general population, without any consideration for race

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is seen in African-American patients with multiple myeloma compared with White patients?
questions.correct_answer: There is a longer time from symptoms to diagnosis
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Improved survival is seen at the same rate", "They are as likely to be entered into clinical trials", "They are as likely to receive transplants"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is the best approach to address glossitis/thrush associated with GPRC5D inhibition?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Dexamethasone oral solution", "Dronabinol", "Sour citrus before meals"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is the best strategy to increase clinical trial participation among Black patients with multiple myeloma?
questions.correct_answer: Proactively identify clinical trials with broadened eligibility criteria
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Avoid discussions about potential compensation for participating in a clinical trial", "Focus on the potential benefits of the study drug and deemphasize the common side effects when going over consent for a clinical trial", "Refer only those patients who have reliable transportation and a caregiver at home"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is true about smoldering multiple myeloma (SMM)?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Five years after a diagnosis of SMM, the per-year progression rate to MM rapidly increases", "More patients with monoclonal gammopathy of unknown significance progress to active MM than SMM", "More than 80% of patients with SMM progress to MM"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is TRUE regarding bispecific agents in multiple myeloma (MM)?
questions.correct_answer: Teclistamab yielded an ORR of 63% in the MajesTEC-1 trial (NCT04557098), enrolling patients with relapsed/refractory MM
questions.incorrect_answers: ["B-cell maturation antigen is the only target under investigation for bispecific agents in MM", "Elranatamab was associated with an ORR of 12% in the MagnetisMM-3 trial enrolling patients with relapsed/refractory MM"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is true regarding relapse in multiple myeloma (MM)?
questions.correct_answer: Heavily pretreated patients have treatment options with high response rates and durable remissions
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Approximately half of patients treated with standard of care initial therapy will eventually relapse", "Elderly patients have limited treatment options in relapse", "Triple class refractory disease only occurs in patients who have received multiple lines of therapy"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is true regarding risk stratification models for SMM?
questions.correct_answer: There is no single standard model for risk stratification for SMM
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Nearly all patients with high-risk SMM will eventually experience disease progression", "Patients with low-risk SMM never experience disease progression", "Risk stratification is unnecessary, since there are no FDA-approved treatments"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is true regarding the BENEFIT trial which compared isatuximab-VRd to isatuximab with lenalidomide and dexamethasone (Rd) in patients who were ineligible for an autologous stem cell transplant (ASCT)?
questions.correct_answer: The isatuximab-VRd regimen resulted in significantly higher rates of minimal residual disease (MRD) negativity
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Progression-free survival was better in patients treated on the isatuximab-Rd arm", "There was no difference in outcomes between the study arms", "Toxicity was similar between the treatment arms"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is true regarding the current bispecific or trispecific antibodies undergoing evaluation for the treatment of multiple myeloma?
questions.correct_answer: They are designed to bring T-cells into closer proximity of MM cells
questions.incorrect_answers: ["They are engineered individually for each patient", "They are linked to a cytotoxic payload", "They must be administered by an MM specialty facility"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following is true regarding the results of the phase 3 study evaluating stem cell mobilization after treatment with plerixafor and granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (G-CSF) vs placebo and G-CSF in patients with multiple myeloma (MM) or non–Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL)?
questions.correct_answer: Patients with NHL and MM mobilized significantly more CD34+ cells on the plerixafor-containing arm
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Patients with MM mobilized significantly more CD34+ cells on the plerixafor-containing arm, but there was no difference for patients with NHL", "Patients with NHL mobilized significantly more CD34+ cells on the plerixafor-containing arm, but there was no difference for patients with MM", "There was no difference between the study arms"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following monoclonal antibodies was previously available under a REMS program, which may require patient education regarding monitoring for corneal AEs if it receives FDA approval again?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Ado-trastuzumab emtansine", "Enfortumab vedotin", "Loncastuximab tesirine"]

questions.correct_answer: S2209: Daratumumab triplet with double- or single-agent maintenance in transplant-ineligible newly diagnosed MM (NDMM)
questions.incorrect_answers: ["CARTITUDE-5: Ciltacabtagene autoleucel in transplant-eligible NDMM", "LINKER-MM3: Linvoseltamab in relapsed/refractory MM", "MagnetisMM-6: Elranatamab in transplant-ineligible NDMM"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following statements best describes safety findings from clinical trials of emerging therapies for relapsed/refractory (R/R) MM?
questions.correct_answer: Combining selinexor with bortezomib at doses lower than those approved by the FDA has been shown to reduce gastrointestinal toxicity
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Bispecific monoclonal antibodies tend to cause more frequent and more severe episodes of cytokine release syndrome than seen with chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapies", "Skin toxicity associated with idecabtagene vicleucel appears to be an adverse effect that is unique to this agent", "Withholding doses of belantamab mafodotin rarely helps to resolve treatment-associated keratopathy"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following statements best summarizes key findings from the phase 3 IMROZ and BENEFIT trials evaluating Isa-VRd for transplant-ineligible patients with NDMM?
questions.correct_answer: Isa-VRd led to significant improvements in PFS compared with VRd in IMROZ, and rates of MRD negativity compared to Isa-Rd in BENEFIT
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Isa-VRd led to significant improvements in PFS and OS compared to VRd and Isa-Rd", "Isa-VRd was associated with substantially higher rates of peripheral neuropathy compared to VRd in IMROZ", "Isa-VRd was associated with substantially higher rates of pneumonia compared to Isa-Rd in BENEFIT"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following statements best summarizes the findings from the IFM 2009, DETERMINATION, and Cardamon trials comparing autologous stem cell transplant (ASCT) to triplet consolidation regimens for patients with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma?
questions.correct_answer: ASCT significantly improves PFS and rates of minimal residual disease (MRD) negativity
questions.incorrect_answers: ["ASCT does not significantly improve PFS", "ASCT significantly improves PFS only in patients with high-risk cytogenetic abnormalities", "ASCT significantly improves primary progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS)"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following statements correctly describes the efficacy outcomes from the CARTITUDE-2 study evaluating cilta-cel in patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma?
questions.correct_answer: Patients with high-risk disease had ~100% response to cilta-cel; however, these patients had shorter PFS than the overall study population

questions.question_stem: Which of the following statements correctly summarizes a key takeaway from available data regarding the impact of MRD status?
questions.correct_answer: Patients who achieve MRD negativity have improved outcomes irrespective of therapy used
questions.incorrect_answers: ["MRD-positive patients who receive anti-CD38 mAb triplet therapy have better outcomes than MRD-negative patients who receive doublet therapy", "Patients who have resurgence of MRD positivity during maintenance therapy have improved outcomes when they are treated for relapse", "Patients with 2 or more high-risk cytogenetic abnormalities who achieve MRD negativity have similar outcomes to those with standard risk disease"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the addition of daratumumab monoclonal antibody (mAb) to maintenance lenalidomide post-consolidation compared with lenalidomide alone in the phase 3 PERSEUS study?
questions.correct_answer: There was a significantly greater increase in MRD-negativity rates during maintenance in the daratumumab-lenalidomide arm
questions.incorrect_answers: ["Rates of MRD negativity declined during maintenance in both arms", "There was no difference in MRD-negativity rates between the study arms", "There was no difference in sustained MRD-negativity rates between the study arms"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Rates of MRD-negativity declined during maintenance in both arms", "The daratumumab-containing arm had improved MRD-negativity rates post-consolidation only", "There was no difference in MRD-negativity rates between the study arms"]

questions.question_stem: Which of the following treatments are more frequently administered to White and Black patients compared with Hispanic and Asian patients?

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Asian, Black, and Hispanic patients", "Black patients only", "Hispanic patients only"]

questions.question_stem: You are a research nurse coordinating enrollment for a patient with relapsed multiple myeloma eligible for bispecific antibody therapy. Which of the following best exemplifies your role in supporting shared decision-making and safe trial participation?

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Diarrhea", "Infusion reaction", "Pneumonia"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Diarrhea", "Fatigue", "Renal failure"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["1 to 5 days", "14 to 28 days", "Within 24 hours"]

questions.question_stem: Your 56-year-old patient with triple class refractory MM is considering treatment with cilta-cel. questions.incorrect_answers: ["1 to 5 days", "10 to 14 days", "Within 24 hours"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Blurry vision", "Depression", "Nausea and vomiting"]

questions.correct_answer: It is “off-the-shelf” so it can be started right after insurance approval

questions.incorrect_answers: ["CD30-negative disease was associated with longer survival than CD30-positive disease", "CD30-positive disease was associated with longer survival than CD30-negative disease"]

questions.incorrect_answers: ["Allo-HSCT", "Another Car-T", "I don\u2019t know"]

questions.question_stem: You are seeing a 71-year-old woman for management of mantle cell lymphoma. questions.incorrect_answers: ["Adding acalabrutinib but not ibrutinib to BR will improve her OS relative to BR alone", "Adding ibrutinib or acalabrutinib to BR will improve her OS relative to BR alone", "Adding ibrutinib to RCHOP/RDHAP+ASCT will improve her OS relative to RCHOP/RDHAP+ASCT alone", "Adding zanubrutinib to BR will improve her OS relative to zanubrutinib/rituximab alone"]

questions.question_stem: The double-blind, phase 3 SHINE study compared first-line treatment with ibrutinib plus bendamustine/rituximab to placebo plus bendamustine/rituximab in newly diagnosed, older patients with mantle cell lymphoma. At a median follow up of 84.7 months, by how long did the PFS in the ibrutinib + bendamustine/rituximab group exceed that of in the placebo + bendamustine/rituximab group?
questions.incorrect_answers: ["0.3 year", "1.3 years", "3.3 years"]

questions.question_stem: Which statement accurately reflects the current role of clinical prognostic tools or biomarkers to guide treatment in FL?

questions.question_stem: A 64-year-old man was diagnosed in 2021 with DLBCL, non-GCB, and was treated off-study with sequential R-CHOP x 4 followed by RICE x 3 with a PET-negative complete response. Therapy was completed 06/2021. In 05/2022, the patient presented complaining of abdominal pain. On exam there was a palpable abdominal mass >10 cm. CBC was unremarkable. CMP was remarkable for K 5.4, Cr 1.5, BUN 28, Ca 9.7. PO4 WNL. Uric acid 9.6. FDG-PET scan confirmed a large 12 x 14 cm mesenteric mass, SUV 16.4, and multiple osseous lesions. The patient was emergently admitted for management of the spontaneous tumor lysis and for initiation of therapy. IR guided biopsy performed after admission confirmed DLBCL, non-GCB with EXPRESSION of CD19, CD20, BCL6, MUM1/IRF4 and NO EXPRESSION of CD10. The patient received 2 cycles of R-DHAX and had a repeat FDG-PET demonstrating resolution of the FDG-avid osseous disease. There is a residual mesenteric mass measuring 5.5 x 4 cm, SUV 5.6. Retrospective analysis from the CIMBTR demonstrated which of the following?
questions.correct_answer: Outcome with high dose therapy and autologous stem cell transplant and CD19-directed CAR T-cell are similar with 1-year PFS of ~55%
questions.incorrect_answers: ["CD19-CAR T-cell therapy is expected to have a PFS at 1 year of >80%", "Consolidation with high dose therapy and autologous stem cell rescue is associated with PFS of <20%"]
